_what: Would the ENGINE's mechanical HOLD survive J's right-thesis shake-outs?

_honesty.EXACT: SPY adverse-poke distances, structural swing levels, CLOSE-based chart-stop breach verdict (his real fills + EXACT SPY 5m path)
_honesty.ESTIMATE: option-% of the poke, the -50% cap SPY-equivalent, captured-recovery $ (BS, IV implied from J's own entry premium)
_honesty.scope: right-thesis shake-outs ONLY (continued_his_way==True AND pnl<0); the engine cannot make a wrong-thesis trade win

adverse_poke_distribution._note: option_pct is NEGATIVE (a drawdown). median ~ how deep the modeled premium sank at the worst adverse tick before the reversal.

which_component_does_the_work._q: if ONLY the chart-stop existed, how many survive? if ONLY the -50% cap?
which_component_does_the_work._read: the component that survives MORE pokes is doing the holding work. The combined engine exits at whichever is NEARER (fires first).

close_confirmation_value._q: production chart-stop is CLOSE-based; how many shake-outs were WICK-only (intrabar poke pierced the structural level but the bar closed back inside, so the engine did NOT exit)?
close_confirmation_value._read: these are trades the close-confirmation rule SAVED from a wick stop-out — direct evidence that 'wait for the 5m bar CLOSE, not the wick' is already the right rule and is load-bearing.

captured_recovery_EST._note: captured = (engine realized px - his exit px) * 100 * qty. HELD cases ride to a HONEST capped exit: 2/3 at TP1 +50%, 1/3 runner at the chandelier-trailed (15% off HWM) favorable extreme — NOT the perfect peak. SHARED cases ~ match his loss at the engine stop.

j_shakeout_buffer_sweep._q: if the chart-stop buffer were wider, how many MORE of J's right-thesis shake-outs would the engine HOLD through (CLOSE-based, structural level + buffer)?
j_shakeout_buffer_sweep._note: monotonic: wider buffer holds more of J's pokes. But see the live-book OOS check below before proposing any change.

live_book_oos_no_regression._method: live params.json forced to real OPRA fills, vary ONLY level_stop_buffer_dollars; full 2025-01-01..2026-05-29 + OOS 2026-03-01..2026-05-29 + J-anchor 2026-04-29..05-07. Mirrors analysis/recommendations/chart-stops-ab-2026-06-18.json method.
live_book_oos_no_regression.verdict: NO-OP on the live book: full +$10,553 / OOS +$2,108 / edge_capture +$1,340 are IDENTICAL at 0.50, 0.75, 1.00 (exit histogram byte-identical). PASSES no-regression trivially because ZERO live trades exit via the chart/level stop — the live book's binding exits are the -50% premium cap (11), time-stop (4), and TP1-then-runner (11). The chart-stop buffer therefore CANNOT hurt the live +EV book; its only effect is on J-style first-poke shake-outs.
live_book_oos_no_regression.honest_caveat: Because the widening is a no-op on the live book, it does NOT IMPROVE the live book either. Its value is specific to J's capitulation pattern. Ship-grade evidence for the live book is the close-confirmation rule (already production) + the wide -50% cap (already shipped, chart-stops-ab-2026-06-18).

companion_premium_stop_evidence.finding: the live-book bear premium-stop sweep already proved WIDER stops are flat-to-better: -0.10 live total $8,160 -> -0.15 peak $11,016 -> -0.50 cap $10,553 (SHIPPED). edge_capture invariant +$1,340 at every width. This is the live-book analogue of J's 'hold through the poke' — already shipped.

chart_stop_buffer_is_dominated_on_live_b._finding: VARY-AND-ASSERT (C14 dead-knob guard): on the current production book, the chart/LEVEL stop NEVER fires at ANY buffer value (tested 0.01, 0.50, 5.00 -> 0 EXIT_ALL_LEVEL_STOP in all). The level-stop is DOMINATED by the ribbon-flip-back exit, which uses the SAME $0.50 price buffer (RIBBON_FLIP_PRICE_BUFFER) plus an opposite 30c ribbon stack and fires first, and by the -50% premium cap.
chart_stop_buffer_is_dominated_on_live_b.implication: Proposing to widen chart_stop_buffer_dollars is a NO-OP on the live book (confirms the trivial OOS pass above). The de-facto 'chart invalidation' in production is the ribbon-flip-back (opposite stack + 30c + $0.50 price move) + the wide -50% cap + the CLOSE-confirmation mechanic, NOT the standalone level-stop buffer.
chart_stop_buffer_is_dominated_on_live_b.what_this_means_for_J: the mechanical-HOLD value proposition is REAL and already shipped via ribbon-flip-back + -50% cap + close-confirmation; the J-shake-out buffer sweep is doctrine VALIDATION (hold direction is correct), not a new live knob to turn.

production_accurate_hold_bound._what: The live engine's de-facto chart invalidation is the ribbon-flip-back (RIBBON_FLIP_PRICE_BUFFER=$0.50 close move past entry + opposite 30c ribbon stack), NOT the never-firing structural level-stop. This bound tests the PRICE gate alone (we cannot reconstruct the ribbon stack from SPY-only bars).
production_accurate_hold_bound._why_conservative: production ALSO requires the opposite 30c stack, so it exits FEWER times than the price-gate alone => real production HOLDS MORE than these numbers. This is a LOWER bound on holds.

two_bounds_summary.structural_swing_chart_stop_model: 62.5% held (the doctrine 'structural invalidation level' interpretation; my primary model)
two_bounds_summary.ribbon_flip_price_gate_conservative: 36.7% held at the live $0.50 (LOWER bound; real production holds more because it also needs the 30c stack)
two_bounds_summary.read: the TRUE production hold-rate sits BETWEEN these (likely 45-60%): the level-stop never binds, the ribbon-flip needs BOTH a $0.50 close-move AND an opposite 30c stack. Either way a CLEAR MAJORITY-to-PLURALITY of J's shake-outs would be HELD, and the close-confirmation mechanic is load-bearing in BOTH.
